mission.title: Install Keylime Attestation Operator on Kubernetes
mission.description: Keylime is a TPM-based remote boot attestation and runtime integrity measurement system. The Keylime Attestation Operator deploys Keylime components (registrar, verifier, and agent) on Kubernetes to provide hardware-rooted trust and continuous node integrity monitoring using TPM 2.0 devices.

steps.title: Clone the attestation-operator repository
steps.description:
Clone the Keylime attestation-operator repository which contains the Helm charts and manifests for deploying Keylime on Kubernetes:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/keylime/attestation-operator.git
cd attestation-operator
```

steps.title: Install cert-manager (prerequisite)
steps.description:
The attestation operator requires cert-manager for TLS certificate management:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/latest/download/cert-manager.yaml
kubectl wait --for=condition=Available --timeout=120s deployment/cert-manager -n cert-manager
kubectl wait --for=condition=Available --timeout=120s deployment/cert-manager-webhook -n cert-manager
```

steps.title: Deploy the Keylime Attestation Operator
steps.description:
Use the Makefile target to deploy the operator and Keylime components via Helm:
```bash
make helm-keylime-deploy
```
This installs the operator CRDs, the operator itself, and creates a default Keylime CR that deploys the registrar, verifier, and agent components.

Alternatively, install step by step:
```bash
make install           # Install CRDs
make deploy            # Deploy the operator
kubectl apply -f config/samples/  # Create Keylime CR
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all Keylime components are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keylime
```
You should see pods for:
- `keylime-registrar` — tracks enrolled agents
- `keylime-verifier` — performs attestation checks
- `keylime-agent` (DaemonSet) — runs on each node with a TPM device
- The attestation operator controller

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Keylime operator, registrar, verifier, and agent pods running in the keylime namespace. Agent pods run as a DaemonSet on nodes with TPM 2.0 devices. Use `kubectl get keylime -n keylime` to check the custom resource status.

uninstall.title: Remove Keylime components
uninstall.description:
Undeploy Keylime using the Makefile:
```bash
make helm-keylime-undeploy
```
Or manually:
```bash
kubectl delete -f config/samples/
make undeploy
make uninstall
```

uninstall.title: Clean up namespace
uninstall.description:
If the namespace still exists after undeployment:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace keylime
```

upgrade.title: Pull latest and redeploy
upgrade.description:
Update the attestation-operator repository and redeploy:
```bash
cd attestation-operator
git pull origin main
make helm-keylime-deploy
```

troubleshooting.title: Agent pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Keylime agents require TPM 2.0 hardware on worker nodes. If agent pods fail to start:
1. Verify TPM device exists: `ls /dev/tpm*` on the node
2. Check agent logs: `kubectl logs -n keylime -l app=keylime-agent`
3. On Kubernetes 1.26+, unprivileged TPM access may be available; on older versions, the agent may need privileged access

troubleshooting.title: cert-manager not ready
troubleshooting.description:
If the operator fails to start with certificate errors, ensure cert-manager is fully ready:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager
kubectl wait --for=condition=Available deployment/cert-manager-webhook -n cert-manager --timeout=120s
```

troubleshooting.title: CRD not found errors
troubleshooting.description:
If you see CRD-related errors, ensure the CRDs were installed:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep keylime
```
If missing, run `make install` to install them.

prerequisites.description: Kubernetes 1.24+ cluster with worker nodes that have TPM 2.0 hardware. cert-manager must be installed. git, make, helm, and kubectl must be available on your workstation.
